Badger Grapple Red — v20.2 Menu Redesign

Built directly from concept-art reference Tony provided (a marketing/
feature-showcase mockup, not a literal engine screenshot — see note
below on that distinction). Rebuilt the Start menu to match its
structure using only real, existing game data:

- Left panel: icon-prefixed list (Wrestlers / Bag / Rosterdex / Badges /
  Practice / Objectives / Save / Options), selection highlight, chevron.
  Icons are small vector glyphs drawn with Phaser Graphics — no new
  art assets needed.
- Right panel: lead wrestler portrait (reuses existing portrait_* art),
  name/level/style, a 4-circle badge tracker (W Badge, Neutral Badge,
  Scramble Badge, Top Badge — the 4 real badges this game has), Day/
  Period, and Grit/Rep readouts.
- Two new tabs that didn't exist before: BAG (lists actual item counts
  and what each does) and BADGES (each badge's name, source area, and
  earned/not-earned state).
- OPTIONS tab now exists and houses the erase-save-data flow (previously
  a top-level "RESET" button sitting next to "SAVE" — moved somewhere a
  destructive action shouldn't be one misclick away, which is also just
  more correct than before).

No new save fields, no gameplay/balance changes. Verified booting clean
in headless Chromium (zero console/page errors) before shipping, all
6 tabs screenshotted.

IMPORTANT CONTEXT — read before the next art pass:
The two images Tony shared as "what I mean by a real game" are concept /
marketing-collage art (a multi-panel feature-showcase image, styled like
a store page), not a screenshot of any engine actually running. That
image's fidelity — readable banner text, a modeled punching bag, painterly
character shading, a bronze statue — is above what 16-24px GBA-scale tile
art can produce; it's closer to a higher-resolution illustrated style.
The ENGINE itself doesn't block that fidelity (it already composites full
painted per-area background images, not raw tiles — see area_*.png), so
the gap is specifically in the ART, not the code. This menu redesign
proves the UI/layout side can already match that reference; closing the
rest of the gap means getting background/character art redone at that
quality into the SAME asset slots (documented dimensions/filenames
unchanged), which is a real, sustained art effort — not a quick pass.

Cache: ?v=202. Proof: Start menu shows icon list + stat card.
